scenario: An online retailer running Camunda 8.8 modernizes 8 microservices that still configure camunda.client.zeebe.base-url to reach the gateway. The team must replace this legacy alias with the documented top-level address property in the Spring Boot starter reference so REST clients resolve the cluster endpoint correctly on 8.8.
question: Which property replaces the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.base-url in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Set camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address to carry the cluster endpoint URL on the client at startup
Set camunda.client.cloud.base-url to carry the cluster endpoint URL on the client at startup
Set camunda.client.rest-address to carry the cluster endpoint URL on the client at startup
Set camunda.client.auth.base-url to carry the cluster endpoint URL on the client at startup

a.text: Incorrect. camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address is itself listed as deprecated and its documented replacement is camunda.client.grpc-address; the base-url replacement is the REST address property.

b.text: Incorrect. The cloud subtree carries cluster identifiers such as cluster-id and region; the base-url alias replacement is the top-level camunda.client.rest-address, not a cloud property.

c.text: Correct. The reference table lists camunda.client.rest-address as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.base-url property in Camunda 8.8. The zeebe.rest-address alias also maps to camunda.client.rest-address.

d.text: Incorrect. The auth subtree holds OIDC fields such as audience and scope; the base-url replacement is camunda.client.rest-address, not an auth property.

explanation: The zeebe subtree under camunda.client is deprecated. Its base-url and rest-address aliases both map to the top-level camunda.client.rest-address, and other connection aliases follow the same pattern, for example camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address mapping to camunda.client.grpc-address.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
